Write a quicksort in TypeScript.

Here is a simple implementation:

function quicksort(arr: number[]): number[] {
  if (arr.length <= 1) return arr;
  const [p, ...rest] = arr;
  const less = rest.filter(x => x < p);
  const more = rest.filter(x => x >= p);
  return [...quicksort(less), p, ...quicksort(more)];
}

Note: this is not in-place.

Make it in-place.

Sure:

function quicksortInPlace(arr: number[], lo = 0, hi = arr.length - 1): void {
  if (lo >= hi) return;
  const pivot = arr[hi]!;
  let i = lo;
  for (let j = lo; j < hi; j++) {
    if (arr[j]! < pivot) {
      [arr[i], arr[j]] = [arr[j]!, arr[i]!];
      i++;
    }
  }
  [arr[i], arr[hi]] = [arr[hi]!, arr[i]!];
  quicksortInPlace(arr, lo, i - 1);
  quicksortInPlace(arr, i + 1, hi);
}
